meta.purpose: Frontend component + state matrix for the tumor board deliverable. Two cases, biology-first hierarchy, honesty inline (not hidden behind a 'degraded' tab). Every component wires directly against the live pydantic schemas in src/oncology_arbiter/api/schemas.py — nothing invented.

cases_covered.claim: The arbiter reasons end-to-end from CT bytes to Elo tournament: L1 CT ingestion → L2 MONAI RetinaNet detection → L3 lung heuristic bucketing → L4 NCCN-lite rule lookup → L5 co-scientist + Elo audit sort. Every layer's model_state is real.

The arbiter is NOT the source of the p-values, effect sizes, or PARP falsification. Those live in the SHA-locked crispro-backend-v2 bundle. The arbiter contributes contract validation, bundle_sha256 audit-of-record, URL honesty gate, and Elo audit sort with visible manuscript-derived weights.

invariants_frontend_MUST_honor: RUO strip is always visible at the root — never skeletonized, never hidden.
Every claim-bearing block carries data-ruo="true" and a model_state chip.
Every therapy view renders a non-dismissible Human Decision footer.
model_state chip color-coding is enforced: loaded_* = green, proxy_* = amber, template = grey.
For the AK case: no proxy_co_scientist chip on manuscript-anchored blocks. The only proxy chip on the AK page is on the Elo audit-sort panel.
For the NSCLC case: the LUNA16 low-confidence signal (top_score=0.042 on an out-of-distribution 41.5 mm mass) is surfaced inline — NOT hidden behind a 'debug' tab.
Zero fake NCT numbers. All ClinicalTrials.gov references are real live search URLs.
Zero placeholder text. Every rendered value comes from receipts/*.json.

shared_components.text: RESEARCH USE ONLY — this deliverable is a UI mock over live API calls; nothing here is validated for clinical decision-making.

shared_components.role: inline chip surfaced next to every claim-bearing block
non-dismissible footer on every therapy/trial view
items: Final therapy selection remains with the treating oncologist.
Enrollment eligibility remains with the trial PI + coordinator.
Tissue biopsy and histopathology remain with the pathologist.
This system reports evidence and audit receipts, not orders.

shared_components.role: shown on any panel derived from co-scientist output

shows: HU planted range: gantry=-1200, lung=-800, body=-50, mass=+50
nsclc_luna16_components.note: L1 is the ingestion contract, not a model — chip is grey.

shows: detection at center (47.5, 73.8, 27.7) mm, diameter=41.5 mm, in_lung=true
nsclc_luna16_components.in_distribution_warning: Detector's low confidence is HONEST — the mass exceeds training range. The lung-heuristic diameter measure drives HIGH bucketing, not the RetinaNet score.

nsclc_luna16_components.note: Amber chip — this layer is a rules-lite heuristic, not a trained ML classifier.

shows: 3 recommended: PET/CT (LUNG-1), CT-guided biopsy (LUNG-2), Tumor board review (Principles Section)
nsclc_luna16_components.note: Amber chip — deterministic rule lookup keyed on the L3 diameter bucket.

nsclc_luna16_components.hero_visualization: baseline vs enriched Elo (per-drug rating chart)
shows: baseline: biopsy #1, tumor board #2, PET/CT #3, surveillance #4
enriched: biopsy #1, PET/CT #2 (↑1), tumor board #3 (↓1), surveillance #4
disease_context echo shows risk_bucket=HIGH driving the modifier choice

ak_mbd4_lof_components.verbatim_text: This case is manuscript-anchored, arbiter validates+audits+surfaces. All p-values, effect sizes, and the PARP falsification come from the SHA-locked crispro-backend-v2 bundle. The arbiter contributes contract validation, bundle_sha256 audit-of-record, URL honesty gate, and a labeled Elo audit sort — nothing more.

ak_mbd4_lof_components.role: first-class row in evidence matrix — NOT quarantined, NOT hidden
ak_mbd4_lof_components.verbatim_narrative: PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) are NOT recommended for MBD4-LOF tumors on a transcriptional-upregulation basis. Competing hypothesis — MBD4-LOF benefits from PARPi via PARP1 transcriptional upregulation — falsified: PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF NOT elevated vs comparator (n=19 vs 1498, p=0.605). Recommended axis is ATR/WEE1 inhibition (lead: ceralasertib).

ak_mbd4_lof_components.shows: 6 rows × 7 modalities table. Empty cells labeled 'missing' — never erased. parp_inhibitors row is first-class alongside atr_wee1, cytidine_analogs, wrn, immunotherapy, pkmyt1.
7 anchor p-values verbatim (6 in atr_wee1 chain + 1 GDSC2 primary). Each row shows p_value, effect_size, n_mut/n_wt, stratifier, PMID URL.
7 drugs. RECOMMENDED (5): cytarabine, decitabine, adavosertib, berzosertib, ceralasertib. NOT_RECOMMENDED_ON_THIS_MECHANISM (2): olaparib, talazoparib.

ak_mbd4_lof_components.role: audit sort with visible labeled weights — NOT a discovery ranking
shows: applied_modifiers labeled by manuscript claim (ceralasertib=lead, olaparib/talazoparib=falsified)
ak_mbd4_lof_components.note: This is the ONLY block on the AK page that carries a proxy chip. Everything else is loaded_*.

shows: brief description of each case's role
